_risk_level_doc: Single-line predicate change in a command file. No security surface, no API boundary, no schema change. Blast radius is one file, one line.

requirement.original: OOS item C — commit.md Step 7 ambiguous spec candidate. Stage 3 uses a literal grep of the task-id string across all docs/dev/specs/*.md files, which returns false positives. The fix is to tighten the candidate predicate: only a spec file that contains a machine-readable marker `<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->` should be a candidate.
requirement.clarified: Replace `grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md` at commands/commit.md:157 with `grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" candidate.md`. Also update the prose description on the same line from 'literal-task-id grep' to 'machine-readable marker grep'. No other changes.
requirement.what: Single grep pattern replacement at commands/commit.md line 157
requirement.why: Prose references to a task-id in historical cross-references cause false-positive stage-3 candidates, which trigger the fail-closed multi-candidate block unnecessarily

included: commands/commit.md line 157 — grep fragment replacement + prose update
excluded: Retroactive marker injection into existing spec files
Any other file or line in commands/commit.md

success_criteria: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md returns no match
grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns no match
commands/commit.md line 157 contains the marker pattern and prose says 'machine-readable marker grep'
commands/commit.md still contains 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' at line 162 (multi-candidate fail-closed preserved)
commands/commit.md still contains 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' at line 161 (single-candidate fail-closed preserved)

grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns empty (exit 1) — stable recipe pinning specific task-id and specific false-positive files
constraints: Marker string is `<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->` — exact format as specified by user

root_cause_analysis.symptom: In the 20260519-211515 cycle, both spec-20260518-225715.md and spec-20260520-221059.md matched stage-3 because both contain prose references to '20260519-211515', triggering the fail-closed multi-candidate block even though neither was the continuation spec.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: The stage-3 predicate `grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md` matches any occurrence of the task-id string, including prose citations, cross-references, and historical notes — it has no semantic discrimination.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — the predicate was introduced as part of the AC5 fix in cycle 20260519-211515 (commit 1a1d058); the false-positive behavior was a known limitation documented in spec-20260520-221059.md:87
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The bare task-id grep was the simplest stage-3 fallback predicate when AC5 was written; explicit marker support was deferred as 'could-have'.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Specs that reference prior cycle task-ids in cross-references (spec-20260518-225715.md) or retrospective analysis (spec-20260520-221059.md) become false-positive candidates, causing the fail-closed block to fire on every commit where those task-ids appear in prose.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced in cycle 20260519-211515 AC5 fix; documented as issue D2 in spec-20260520-221059.md:87 on 2026-05-21.
observations: commands/commit.md:157 contains `grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md` (read directly in this session)
docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md:87 records: 'D2 — Step 7 false positive: spec-20260518-225715.md contains a "20260519-211515" historical cross-reference' (read directly)
grep of '20260519-211515' in spec-20260518-225715.md returns 3 matches at lines 233, 235, 245 (all prose/historical) (measured)
grep of '20260519-211515' in spec-20260520-221059.md returns 7 matches at lines 87, 90, 91, 132, 133, 134, 181 (all prose/retrospective) (measured)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Read commands/commit.md:157 directly; confirmed the grep predicate is `grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md`. Grepped both false-positive spec files and confirmed they contain only prose references, no marker.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Stage 3 uses a literal grep of the task-id string … which returns false positives
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->' replaces bare task-id grep at line 157

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: only a spec file that contains a machine-readable marker explicitly declaring itself a continuation spec for that task-id should be a candidate
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Marker-bearing spec IS returned as candidate; prose-only spec is NOT

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: The fail-closed behavior (multiple candidates → no auto-pick, block commit) is CORRECT and must be preserved
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Multi-candidate outcome block (line 162) in commands/commit.md is unchanged; single-candidate outcome block (line 161) is also unchanged — AC3 and AC3b

component_chain.role: Step 7 stage-3: glob + content predicate for spec candidate discovery

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md — this is the ONLY content predicate allowed; no other grep, no free-form content scan
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Restricts stage-3 to a single deterministic content predicate; prevents implementers from adding secondary greps

setup.reason: non-UI -- config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: In the 20260519-211515 cycle, both spec-20260518-225715.md and spec-20260520-221059.md matched stage-3 because both mention task-id 20260519-211515 in prose, triggering the fail-closed multi-candidate block.

expected.source: User requirement document (verbatim marker format specification)

evidence.gap: Current predicate matches any string occurrence; expected predicate matches only explicit machine-readable marker. The two false-positive specs (spec-20260518-225715.md, spec-20260520-221059.md) contain 3 and 7 prose matches respectively but zero marker occurrences.

reference_source.description: commands/commit.md read directly in this session; false-positive evidence from spec-20260520-221059.md:87 and grep results on spec-20260518-225715.md

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt exists for this specific predicate change

context.codebase_state: commands/commit.md Step 7 stage-3 uses bare task-id grep at line 157
context.recent_commits: 1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation (introduced the stage-3 algorithm including the bare grep)

development_approach.strategy: Single-line edit at commands/commit.md:157: replace the grep fragment and update the prose label 'literal-task-id grep' to 'machine-readable marker grep'
development_approach.validation_approach: Static grep checks: (1) grep -lF of marker for task-id 20260519-211515 against the two specific false-positive specs (spec-20260518-225715.md, spec-20260520-221059.md) returns exit 1 — stable, pins specific task-id + specific files; (2) commands/commit.md line 157 contains the new marker pattern; (3) 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' still present at line 162 (multi-candidate fail-closed preserved, AC3); (4) 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' still present at line 161 (single-candidate fail-closed preserved, AC3b)

research_findings.reason: Straightforward single-line predicate replacement with a user-specified marker format; no architectural decision required

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Round 1 (original spec): Codex confirmed no blocking issues; one in-scope PROPOSED_FIX (prose update on line 157 from 'literal-task-id grep' to 'machine-readable marker grep'). Round 2 (QA remediation): Codex validated two proposed AC fixes for OBJ-1 and OBJ-2. Both fixes confirmed correct and stable. No blocking issues found. Note: Bash write to /var/tmp blocked by BA tool policy; consultation conducted via Skill(codex) and self-review of logical correctness per the graceful-fallback protocol.

findings.issue: No blocking issues with the proposed fix
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed the fix is correct and minimal

findings.issue: Stale wording on line 157: if only the grep fragment is replaced, the prose 'literal-task-id grep' becomes inaccurate
findings.proposed_fix: Update the same line's prose to say 'machine-readable marker grep'. Do not touch other stages or files.
findings.rationale: In-scope: line 157 is already the change target. The prose must accurately describe the new predicate. Added as AC5 and incorporated into Technical Hints.

findings.issue: Marker producer absence: stage 3 returns empty unless a marker already exists in a spec
findings.rationale: Intentional per user requirement and AC4. Future BA cycle should add marker-placement instructions to the spec-creation workflow.

findings.issue: OBJ-1: AC3 covered only line 162 (multi-candidate fail-closed). Line 161 (single-candidate fail-closed) also structurally affected by predicate change — needs AC3b
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC3b: GIVEN exactly one marker-bearing spec, WHEN stage 3 runs, THEN single-candidate branch (line 161) fires with 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' and exit non-zero. Verification: grep -nF of that string in commit.md returns >=1 match.
findings.rationale: QA objection OBJ-1 is correct. Line 161 and 162 both feed from the candidate set produced by the predicate; both need coverage. AC3b added.

findings.issue: OBJ-2: AC4 verification recipe 'grep -rF spec-continuation-of docs/dev/specs/' is time-transient — exits 0 once any future spec ships a marker, causing the AC to appear to fail
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite to: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns empty (exit 1). Stable because it pins the specific incident task-id and the specific false-positive files; future specs use different task-ids.
findings.rationale: QA objection OBJ-2 is correct. The rewritten recipe is stable and directly verifies the incident scenario without being affected by future spec additions.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Round 1: Applied prose update AC5. Round 2 (QA remediation): Applied OBJ-1 fix (added AC3b covering single-candidate branch line 161) and OBJ-2 fix (rewrote AC4 verification recipe to pin specific task-id and specific false-positive files for permanent stability).
